explanation: GaussianNB assumes conditional independence of features given the class. Marginal correlations show some inter-feature dependence (e.g., P↔K: r=0.736). However, class-conditional mean |r| = 0.081, which is low. GaussianNB performs well (99.49%) because strong class separability reduces reliance on joint feature distributions — when classes are well-separated in feature space, even a naive independence assumption suffices for classification.
